front: Play: the curmudgeonly Knemon accepts Sostratos as a son-in-law after being rescued from a well; prologue by Pan
Play: only near-complete text found on a Bodmer papyrus in Egypt
Play: misunderstandings over a baby between Demeas, Chrysis, and others end in the marriage of Moschion and Plangon
Playwright: Aristophanes of Byzantium asked of this author and life, 'which of you imitated the other?'
Playwright: originated 'He whom the gods love dies young' and 'Bad company corrupts good character' (quoted by Saint Paul)
Playwright: the Sappho legend of jumping off a rock after rejection by Phaon first appeared in a play by this author
